headline: Death penalty moratorium covers 1,337 inmates since 2018
context: Malaysia's moratorium on executions, in place since 2018, applies to 1,337 death row inmates as Parliament debates the Abolition of Mandatory Death Penalty Act.

cards.big: 1,337 inmates await resentencing after 2023 mandatory death penalty abolition
cards.sub: Courts must individually review each case under new sentencing guidelines

cards.big: Abolition of Mandatory Death Penalty Act 2023 replaced 11 capital offences
cards.sub: Judges now have discretion to impose life imprisonment or 30-40 year terms

cards.big: Amnesty International recorded 1,342 global executions in 2024 across 16 nations
cards.sub: Malaysia joined 106 countries that have abolished mandatory death sentences

cards.big: 78% of death row inmates were convicted of drug offences under DDA 1952
cards.sub: Lower-income communities disproportionately represented on death row

cards.big: Reform passed, but 1,337 people still wait years for resentencing hearings
Only 186 of 1,337 cases resentenced by end of 2025 due to court backlog
